name: Belmez and Sierra Boyera Reservoir Loop
name_original: Bélmez — circular por el Embalse de Sierra Boyer
description: Hike the moderate 6.3-mile (10.2 km) circular route around Embalse de Sierra Boyera, enjoying reservoir views and walking across the dam.
region_intro: Hiking trails around Belmez traverse a landscape characterized by rolling hills, historical sites, and the presence of the Sierra Boyera Reservoir. The region features a mix of open terrain and greenways, such as the Vía Verde del Guadiato and Los Pedroches. Elevation changes are generally moderate, making many routes accessible for various fitness levels. This area offers a blend of natural scenery and cultural points of interest. Best hiking trails around Belmez The most popular hiking route is Bélmez from Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo — circular by the Vía Verde del Guadiato and Los Pedroches, a 11.2 miles (18.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 44 minutes to complete. This moderate circular walking route follows a former railway line. Another top favourite among local hikers is Belmez and Sierra Boyera Reservoir Loop, a moderate 6.3 miles (10.2 km) path. This route offers views of the Sierra Boyera Reservoir and surrounding terrain. Local hikers also love the Belmez Castle loop from Belmez, a 4.4 miles (7.1 km) trail leading through historical areas and local hills, often completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes. Hiking around Belmez is defined by greenways, reservoir paths, and routes around historical hills. The network offers options for different ability levels, including easy hikes near Belmez. The routes in Belmez are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Belmez's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Belmez?
A: Yes, Belmez offers several easy hiking trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20121979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belmez Castle loop from Belmez&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.4 miles (7.1 km) route that takes you through historical areas and local hills, perfect for a relaxed walk.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Belmez?
A: Hiking trails around Belmez traverse a varied landscape of rolling hills, historical sites, and reservoir paths. You'll find a mix of open terrain and greenways, such as the Vía Verde del Guadiato and Los Pedroches. Elevation changes are generally moderate, making most routes accessible.

Q: Are there any circular walking routes in Belmez?
A: Yes, many of the routes in Belmez are circular, offering convenient starting and ending points. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1248986408/belmez-from-penarroya-pueblonuevo-circular-by-the-via-verde-del-guadiato-and-los-pedroches" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bélmez from Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo — circular by the Vía Verde del Guadiato and Los Pedroches&lt;/a&gt;, which follows a former railway line.

Q: What are the best seasons for hiking in Belmez?
A: The best seasons for hiking in Belmez are typically spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these months, temperatures are milder and more comfortable for outdoor activities. Summers can be very hot, while winters are generally mild but can have occasional rain.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Belmez?
A: Many of the trails in Belmez are suitable for families due to their moderate elevation changes and accessible terrain. Easy routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20121979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belmez Castle loop from Belmez&lt;/a&gt; are great choices for walks with children, offering historical interest without being too strenuous.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Belmez?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in Belmez. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock. Please also ensure you clean up after your pet to maintain the natural environment for everyone.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see while hiking in Belmez?
A: Belmez offers a blend of natural scenery and cultural points of interest. Along your hikes, you might encounter historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5360266" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belmez Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The region also features greenways and views of the Sierra Boyera Reservoir.

Q: Where can I find parking for hiking routes in Belmez?
A: Most hiking routes in Belmez start from or near the town center or other accessible points. You can usually find public parking available in Belmez itself or in nearby villages like Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo, depending on your chosen starting point. Always check local signage for specific parking regulations.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Belmez?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Hikers often praise the blend of natural scenery with historical elements, the well-maintained greenways, and the accessibility of routes for various fitness levels.

Q: Are there any hikes that offer views of the Sierra Boyera Reservoir?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1249053303/belmez-and-sierra-boyera-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belmez and Sierra Boyera Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 6.3 miles (10.2 km) path specifically designed to offer beautiful views of the Sierra Boyera Reservoir and its surrounding terrain.

Q: How long do hiking trails in Belmez typically take to complete?
A: The duration of hikes in Belmez varies depending on the route's length and your pace. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1248986408/belmez-from-penarroya-pueblonuevo-circular-by-the-via-verde-del-guadiato-and-los-pedroches" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bélmez from Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo — circular&lt;/a&gt; typically takes around 4 hours 44 minutes, while the shorter &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20121979" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belmez Castle loop&lt;/a&gt; can be completed in about 1 hour 54 minutes.
tips: It never ceases to amaze me to walk on incredible man-made structures like the dam of the Sierra Boyera reservoir on the Guadiato River.

Belmez Castle is a historic fortress located in Belmez. It stands majestically on a high limestone rock, offering a stunning view of the village and surrounding areas.
The castle dates back to at least 1245, with significant additions made in the 15th century. It played an important role during the Reconquista and was occupied by French troops during the Peninsular War (1810-1812). The castle features a pentagonal Keep, six semicircular towers and a zigzag staircase leading up to it.
If you're planning a visit, it's open daily and admission is free.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the circular route around Embalse de Sierra Boyera?
faqs.answer: The route around the Embalse de Sierra Boyera is a moderate hike that typically takes approximately 2 hours and 44 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this hike?
faqs.answer: This hike is considered to be of moderate difficulty. It requires a good level of fitness but features easily accessible paths, making it suitable for most skill levels. The elevation gain is around 115 meters.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail offers diverse scenery as it circumnavigates the Embalse de Sierra Boyera. You'll encounter rolling and undulating hills with gentle slopes, alongside alluvial plains. A notable part of the route involves walking directly on the impressive Sierra Boyera Reservoir Dam. The area is also known for its aquatic life, particularly birds, adding to the natural beauty.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, beyond the picturesque natural surroundings, a key feature is walking across the impressive Sierra Boyera Reservoir Dam. While not always visible, the reservoir also holds historical significance as an ancient Iberian settlement was discovered at its bottom during a drought in 2017. The nearby Belmez Castle is also a significant local landmark.

faqs.question: Is the Bélmez — circular around the Embalse de Sierra Boyer trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially around wildlife and other hikers, and to clean up after them to maintain the natural environment.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or is there an entrance fee to hike this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the circular route around the Embalse de Sierra Boyera. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed year-round, but spring and autumn are often considered the best seasons due to milder temperatures and pleasant weather. These seasons also offer vibrant natural colors and active birdlife around the reservoir. Summers can be very hot in this region of Spain, so early mornings or late afternoons are advisable if hiking then.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the trailhead is not detailed in the available data. However, as a circular route around a major reservoir, there are typically designated parking areas near the dam or access points in the nearby towns of Belmez, Peñarroya-Pueblonuevo, or Fuente Obejuna. It is advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, approximately 7% of this route overlaps with the Vía Verde del Guadiato y Los Pedroches, offering a chance to experience a section of this longer greenway.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a hike on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the moderate difficulty and potential for sun exposure, it's recommended to bring plenty of water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), comfortable hiking shoes, and appropriate clothing for the weather. Binoculars could also enhance your experience for bird watching around the reservoir.

faqs.question: Is the trail suitable for families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, with its moderate difficulty and easily accessible paths, the trail is generally suitable for families with children who have a good level of fitness. The diverse scenery and opportunity to walk on the dam can be engaging for younger hikers.
